examples.title: Basic tabs
examples.description: Three tabs with associated panels; clicking a tab switches the panel. Uses the Tabs namespace API.

examples.title: Fill pane tabs
examples.description: Tabs can fill a bounded flex pane so the active panel takes the remaining height.
examples.code:
Timeline Logs Metadata

Queued

Provisioned runner

Installed dependencies

Built artifacts

Ran browser checks

Uploaded report

10:01:22 Starting worker

10:01:25 Restoring cache

10:01:31 Running validation

10:02:04 Validation complete

The panel body fills the same bounded pane.

Scrollable content can stay inside the active panel.

The Tabs root owns the flex behavior through the fill prop.

examples.title: Vertical tabs with keyboard navigation
examples.description: A vertical tablist: focus a tab and use ↑/↓ to move. The focus ring must render complete on all sides even though the tab list is a clipping (overflow) container.
examples.code:

examples.title: Tabs with trailing badges
examples.description: Tabs use a decorative `trailing` snippet (wrapped in aria-hidden) for badges and counters. The accessible name only includes the visible label.
examples.code:
Inbox {#snippet trailing()} 3 {/snippet} Drafts {#snippet trailing()} 12 {/snippet} Archive

You have three unread messages.

Twelve drafts waiting for your attention.

Nothing archived recently.

examples.title: Tabs with keyboard navigation
examples.description: Focus a tab and use ←/→ to move; horizontal tablists activate on focus per the WAI-ARIA pattern.
